summary: Task 2 files (CrashHandler, CrashState, Killer) introduce no security findings. Intent to CrashReportActivity is explicit (setClassName with context.packageName + full class FQN, lines 63-66) and cannot be hijacked by another app. All filesystem writes are confined to context.filesDir/logs/ with fixed filename constants; no openFileOutput, no MODE_WORLD_* flags, no WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE, no user-controlled path components. Process.killProcess is invoked with Process.myPid() only. Log.e breadcrumb in the outer catch emits the handler-internal throwable to logcat, which matches tech-spec Flow A step 4 and is accepted under Decision 11 (unfiltered content in Phase 2.5). The exception.message CR/LF scrubbing (lines 79-81) preserves the single-line header contract without enabling injection. No deserialization, no reflection on user input, no shell exec (logcat belongs to Task 3). Decisions 4, 5, 6, 7, 10, 11 honored at the file level; D4 process-guard is correctly deferred to Task 5 per spec. Tests inject a fake Killer and writer, so no risk of abuse through the test seam (internal visibility, production default is DEFAULT_CRASH_LOG_WRITER writing fixed File objects only).
